content.problemStatement:
A pre-requisite to learning Django is working knowledge of basic HTML.

If you are able to build simple static websites such as the one displayed in the browser - then you can continue on your journey to Learning Django for Web development.

In case you feel you want to Learn and revise your concepts of HTML - please review our Practical course on [Basic HTML / CSS](https://www.codechef.com/learn/course/html). \
It will take about 3 - 4 weeks of your time to complete the same.

If you feel you already know HTML but just want to brush up - we would suggest you to practice your HTML skills on our [Projects](https://www.codechef.com/practice/html).
constraints:
- $1 \leq T \leq 100$
- $1 \leq N \leq 100$
- $1 \leq M \leq N\cdot(N-1)/2$
- $1 \leq u_i, v_i \leq N$
- $u_i \neq v_i$ for each $1 \leq i \leq M$.
- The sum of $N$ over all test cases won't exceed $100$.
content.inputFormat:
- The first line of input will contain a single integer $T$, denoting the number of test cases.
- Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.
    - The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers $N$ and $M$ — the number of vertices and edges of the graph, respectively.
    - The next $M$ lines describe the edges. The $i$-th of these $M$ lines contains two space-separated integers $u_i$ and $v_i$, denoting an edge between $u_i$ and $v_i$.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output on a new line the number of good subgraphs of $G$, modulo $10^9 + 7$.
